architecture_fit.note: Minimal 1-layer, no-MLP delta whose hand-built thermometer makes gcd linearly decodable (R2~1); but the attention is largely cosmetic since the residual feature is written in directly rather than computed by the head.

baseline_comparison.note: Explicit strawman: full circuit R2~1/acc=1 vs canonical raw-[a,b] probe at R2~0/acc=0.125, beaten in every gcd bin.

faithfulness.note: Genuine causal ablation zeroing the thermometer subspace collapses decodability to baseline (R2 -0.065, acc 0.031); correct check, though somewhat tautological for a hand-constructed residual.

operating_range.note: MAX_N sweep 10->1000 (2+ orders) with d_model scaled to MAX_N keeps circuit R2~1 while baseline stays flat at 0, no silent failure.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is hand-set torch weights (divisibility -> suffix-OR thermometer) reproducing gcd exactly — exactly what the bonus rewards.

visual_judgement.note: Demo pairs a pred-vs-true y=x scatter (headline R2) with ablation bars and a log-x scale curve — each claim legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties scatter to the linear-decodability headline, ablation bars to faithfulness, and scale curve to operating range, justifying axes and baselines.

notes: A clean hand-built circuit that nails the headline (gcd linearly decodable from the residual at R2~1 vs ~0 baseline) and adds the two things first_pass lacked: a causal ablation that collapses decodability to baseline and a MAX_N sweep across 2+ orders of magnitude. The visualisations are well chosen and the hardcoded-weights bonus fully applies. Main reservation: the attention head is decorative — the thermometer feature is written into the residual directly rather than computed by the mechanism — so the ablation is somewhat tautological, capping architecture fit and faithfulness. Mean of scored items 4.71 -> good.
